Hey guys, welcome to the second installment of seven figure emails. This one is called the Simon Cowell Qualifier. And as usual, this is how it is going to go down. First, we're going to give a little bit of a background on the template, how it came to be, the persuasion principles, why it works, what we're trying to achieve through this template and through using Simon Cowell as our copy coach, I guess. And we'll look at the template itself and then the next video, we will see what it actually looked like in a successful launch before. So a little bit of background. It's kind of impossible to tell the story without giving away my fandom of really cheesy, really cheesy I guess, singing and talent shows. But yes, I'm in Cowell, kind of a fan. But more than my fandom. I always found it fascinating how, yes, he was kind of the biggest jerk on the show. Yes, he would have the snotiest remarks. Yes, a lot of people love to hate him. He was somehow kind of the mainstay on all these shows for well over a decade. Judges came and went, shows came and went, but he kind of remained the main judge in all of this. And I found that super interesting. And what I also found super interesting was how, you know, more so in the last few years, a lot of these clips from these shows, I think America's Got Talent is one of them, would go viral. And a lot of the ones I went viral were the ones that said that really featured Simon having kind of a change of kind of an unexpected reaction, you know, something that blew his mind, something that choked him up, something that made his jaw drop in pure awe. Those are the ones that kind of went the most viral. And there's something powerful in that. Essentially what it's all pointing to is that when you are the harshest judge and have the highest standards and when those standards are transcended and achieved, that creates a really awesome breakthrough. And I was just wondering, how can we actually hold our prospect to a higher, even more impossible standard and give them the joy of having met and surpassed them? Because that's what seems to be lending itself to this judge, you know, being a mainstay on television for the past decade, as well as his videos of his reactions going the most viral. Now this ties back into some things we're going to talk about now. And one of your biggest jobs as a marketer is to enroll your prospect in a better, more successful version of themselves, not the ones they necessarily think they need to be, but the ones they really can be based on who they are, what they've already achieved and what they can achieve with your help. This is one of the most exciting jobs you have as a marketer. And holding them to that standard is tough work, but it's well worth it, especially if you can really nail the part where you're calling them to a game that they've been afraid to call themselves to. So that's what we're doing. We're serving our prospect by calling them to a higher standard, but an achievable standard than they'd be willing to set on their own. So at its core, the Simon Cowell qualifier isn't about insulting our prospects as Simon would on TV. It's really about calling your audience to a higher game. It's about transcending the lower level goals, the safer outcomes that they're used to seeing from your competitors or maybe the ones that they still hold for themselves or the ones they've already achieved and instead inviting them to use your product or service only if they're ready to meet a new standard of success, the one that you've been courageous enough to call them towards. So that's what we're qualifying them based off of. We're qualifying them based off of their willingness and courage to meet you at the bar that you've raised for them in their lives. And that is some powerful stuff. So the persuasion deep dive, the first one is exclusive empowerment and here we're exclusively empowering them based off of lower level outcomes that they've already achieved or higher standards and motivations than most of the market is willing to set for themselves. So this is already saying like you've already achieved all these lower level things. You know, you don't need that anymore. You've already done it. You don't want them to aim higher or saying that you unlike most other people on your market aren't willing to settle for great. You want to go for bloody excellent and I'm joining you in that these are our standards. Let's actually partner up to do it. So that's what we're doing. We're exclusively empowering them based off of things they've already achieved or their willingness to raise the bar with us. Next thing in qualifying them, the distance from the lower level outcomes. So basic social dynamic theory suggests that by maintaining the tone of us qualifying them, they need to meet our standards here. Our prospect will be more likely to agree with the traits that qualify them. They want to ascend to the bar, not degrade themselves further away from it. That's basic social dynamics. So really distancing themselves away from the ones that would disqualify them. And when we write this email and you'll see what the example looks like, we're painting those lower level outcomes, the ones that might be a little bit white, noisy and common in the industry. We're gently degrading them, not in a mean way, just saying like, that's not what we're here to do and that's not what you're here to do. And if we could get them to agree that you're right, that's not what I'm here to do. I could actually aim a little bit higher than that's a powerful dynamic that makes us uniquely equipped to meet them with their new goals and their new standards. This leads us to the next one, awakening dormant desires. So this is actually designed to be a really awesome, fun, pivotal moment in your relationship with your prospect. And more importantly, in their lives and careers or whatever it is you're serving them with, this is where they call themselves to that higher game, where they realize how maybe they've been settling, how they've earned the right based off of what they've already done or what they're capable of to reach or aim a little bit higher. So this is a powerful shift that you get the honor of facilitating with an email that immediately the second they subscribe to that higher bar you've sent and have transcended those lower outcomes, this creates a desire, a desire gap. Sorry. It's the first time they're really awakening this desire. So I have a desire for this new outcome, but I have no idea how to attain it, which makes your offer immediately relevant and attractive. And in a category of one, because they literally haven't tried to solve that problem before achieved that desire. So awakening that desire in this moment makes you literally the only person who can satisfy it. And that is super powerful. More than the persuasion principle of this, there are so few things more mutually exciting for both a marketer and a prospect or a market or a prospect and an end enrollment specialist. Sorry. Jumping slides. Then when they can experience a new desire for the first time with you, that is a pivotal breakthrough that you can facilitate as someone who has devoted themselves to coaching the conversion instead of just pounding them with sales arguments. So that's really what's at play here. And then of course we have the mid-cro commitment. So we spoke about this in the last video. It's kind of a micro commitment, but a little bit stronger, a little bit more tied to your actual offer. And in here, what it is, is we're calling them to a commitment. We're saying, will you actually commit to these bigger outcomes? And why wouldn't they? You know, we've had them distance themselves from those lower level outcomes. We've already said that you're exclusively empowered to actually chase something higher than you've given yourself permission to. You've already achieved these things. You could reach a little bit higher. So why wouldn't they commit to something greater? And that's what we're looking for as a mid-cro commitment, because if they can commit to these new outcomes that they haven't even been seeking an answer for before, then all of a sudden that's a powerful precursor to the main conversion. And then finally it's about establishing consistency with the new outcome. So if they make that mid-cro commitment and agree that they are willing to chase this new bar that you've set together and that they now trust you to actually enable and facilitate because you're the one who awakened that desire in the first place, then it is now your product or service that will act as the bridge between those two realities. So all these principles stack together to create that one effect where they have this brand new desire. They have a gap. They don't know how to get it. They've never sought an answer for this before. And you're here right now to continue facilitating that transformation. So that's how it works. A few questions to elicit the content for this. What has my ideal client already and recently achieved? How do they experience those achievements on every level? Mental, physical, emotional? What's the next level of attainment or outcome that they may not have accepted for themselves yet? So based off of number one, what should they be striving for right now that maybe they haven't been courageous enough to do without somebody coaching them through that process? How would they experience those new outcomes? So paint that picture, future pace, what's possible for them if they set that new standard for them? What are lower level standards the market has for your type of product or service? Now, what are other competitors offering? What are they promising? How are those lower level standards no longer applicable to where your prospect is and what's the higher marker of success you can call them to? That is exciting that they are willing to enroll in because you are first going to enroll them in this new vision of success, whatever industry, whatever that means for your industry as a precursor for them enrolling in your product or service. Where to send it? So this could be both pre-launch, you know, as an invitation to a conversion event, which could be a webinar invite and you'll see what that looks like when we break down the example below or you could take the exact same principles and use this as a standalone sales email template itself, list lower level outcomes, flip the script, say that's not us, that's not what we're about, that's not what we're doing, that's not what you should be doing. This is the higher game we're going to call you to. This is the new list of standards, the higher outcomes we should actually aspire to do contrast the two, let them opt out of those old standards and decide, yes, I'm going to reach with you for these higher ones and the call to action is really framed as like the bridge, you know, time to transcend this and go for that and then qualify them based on their goals, ambitions and desires for that outcome. Say this isn't something that you are willing to strive for. If you're not really ambitious enough to reach for this, then we're probably not the best fit. If you are, let's partner and continue that journey and then you have your final call to action. So all that said, a lot of information, but it gets distilled into a pretty, pretty tight email, which we're going to break down in the video below. So catch me there and I'll see you soon.
